title: celastraceae
intro: The Celastraceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the staff tree or bittersweet family. This family consists of approximately 70-80 genera and over 1,000 species, which are primarily found in tropical and temperate regions around the world. Members of the Celastraceae family are often characterized by their simple or compound leaves, small white or yellow flowers, and woody stems. They are found in a variety of habitats, including forests, woodlands, and grasslands, and many species are valued for their ornamental or medicinal properties.
